name: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Component Object Model Hijacking
notes: This Building Block detects Component Object Model (COM) hijacking activities. The Building Block monitors registry details that may be changed to load a different process (e.g. Command Prompt was modified to execute a malicious exe file instead) followed by a process creation.
bb_references: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Component Object Model Hijacking on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Successful Registry Modification, System.Process Creation Success
when at least 2 events are seen with the same Object Name Lowercase (custom), Machine Identifier (custom) and different Low Level Category in 2 minutes
thresholds: when at least 2 events are seen with the same Object Name Lowercase (custom), Machine Identifier (custom) and different Low Level Category in 2 minutes
TD_list: APPLY BB:BehaviorDefinition: Component Object Model Hijacking on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Successful Registry Modification, System.Process Creation Success
AND when at least 2 events are seen with the same Object Name Lowercase (custom), Machine Identifier (custom) and different Low Level Category in 2 minutes
